question: Write a C++ program that prompts the user to enter the length and width of a rectangle (as floating-point numbers), then calculates and displays its area and perimeter with appropriate labels.

test_cases.expected:
Enter the length of the rectangle: Enter the width of the rectangle: 
--- Calculation Results ---
Area: 22.00
Perimeter: 19.00

Enter the length of the rectangle: Enter the width of the rectangle: 
--- Calculation Results ---
Area: 0.00
Perimeter: 0.00

Enter the length of the rectangle: Enter the width of the rectangle: 
--- Calculation Results ---
Area: 0.00
Perimeter: 0.00

driver: int main() { float length, width; std::cout << "Enter the length of the rectangle: "; std::cin >> length; std::cout << "Enter the width of the rectangle: "; std::cin >> width; float area = length * width; float perimeter = 2 * (length + width); std::cout << "\n--- Calculation Results ---\n"; std::cout << "Area: " << std::fixed << std::setprecision(2) << area << std::endl; std::cout << "Perimeter: " << std::fixed << std::setprecision(2) << perimeter << std::endl; return 0; }
errors: Missing input validation for floating-point numbers
Program can produce incorrect results (e.g., 0.00) or undefined behavior on invalid non-numeric input
Does not handle non-numeric input gracefully, leading to silent failures
